what_it_does: Proposes a de-duplicated fusion design that maps the Company's RHM brain onto OpenWebUI's manifold Pipe surface and its MCP tools onto OpenWebUI's native MCP tool-server surface, traces a live load-induced bridge wedge, records verification results, and lists next-step options.
observations: It deliberately separates design decisions from a live bridge wedge bug.
It recommends exposing the brain as one OWUI manifold Pipe (operator plus registry-driven role/model list) and tools via OWUI's native type:'mcp' gateway to Company :8772.
It recommends retiring the OpenAI-shim adapter :4300 and the parallel channels-operator tool-runner path.
It catalogs three existing seams: S1 OpenAI-shim, S2 owui_room channels-operator, S3 native MCP gateway.
It flags that DEFAULT_BRAIN = deepseek-v4-pro:cloud is still a live fallback at six call-sites.
It includes a dated verification table with commands and results.
It lists four unverified items that block the native-MCP recommendation.
It offers next-step options A through D.
standouts: The clean de-dup proposal: one OWUI Pipe for the brain, one native MCP tool-server for tools, channels keep only timeline value.
The bridge :8770 wedge is load-induced and clears on restart; a durable fix needs per-request deadlines plus a bounded worker pool.
The DEFAULT_BRAIN -pro anti-pattern persists as a silent wrong-brain fallback even though runtime resolves to the 4B.
conventions: Uses YAML front-matter with type, title, subject, posture, created, verified_live, relates-to.
Uses dense inline file:line references and section cross-references.
Separates design reasoning from live-bug diagnosis.
Uses tables for seams and verification results.
Maintains a provisional, verify-first tone with explicit 'correct directly' invitations.
concerns: The native-MCP recommendation depends on unverified :8772 transport-fit and auth-admit.
MCP op counts disagree across sources (80 measured, 66 in remote.py comment, prompt says 68) and remain unresolved.
The bridge wedge mechanism is not root-caused; the proposed durable fix is speculative.
DEFAULT_BRAIN remains a silent wrong-brain fallback at multiple call-sites.
notes: A provisional design proposal for unifying Company brain and tools inside OpenWebUI while honestly flagging unverified blockers and a load-induced bridge wedge.
questions: What exact transport and authentication shape does OWUI's MCP client require for Company :8772?
How will the manifold Pipe preserve voice-call sentence-split TTS behavior?
What is the definitive root cause of the bridge :8770 wedge?
Which MCP op count is authoritative?
purpose_vs_actual: Yes — the file proposes a model+tool seam fusion, traces a live bug, and records verification, matching its stated purpose.
apparent_themes: De-duplication and built-into-the-Company over parallel adapters.
Registry-driven surfaces instead of hand-listed models or tools.
Separation of the brain resolver from the tool gateway.
intention_signals: To converge the Company's RHM brain and governed MCP tools into OpenWebUI's native extension points so the fusion is maintained as one system rather than multiple overlapping bridges.
novelty: Treats the RHM as a source-resolver rather than a model, and proposes a single conversational operator plus a registry-derived manifold of named roles/models, alongside moving tools to OWUI's native MCP surface.
contribution: A de-duplicated seam design mapping brain to an OWUI manifold Pipe and tools to an OWUI native MCP gateway, plus a traced load-induced bridge wedge and a verification checklist that future builders can correct directly.
summary_for_embedding: A proposal document that designs the fusion of Company's RHM brain and MCP tools into OpenWebUI via a manifold Pipe for models and a native MCP tool-server for tools, while tracing a load-induced bridge wedge and recording live verification results.
intention_for_embedding: To decide how the Company's brain and tools should surface inside OpenWebUI without duplication and to flag the blockers and live bugs that must be resolved first.
